nativeRange.habitat: Shallow lagoon reefs with algae-covered rocks and rubble, typically 0-12 m depth; often associated with empty barnacle shells and mollusc tubes on coral outcrops.

decorPreferences: abundant live rock with microalgae growth
sandy substrate with rubble and crevices for perching and refuge
tightly fitted lid to prevent jumping

diet.notes: Primarily a microalgae grazer that browses film and filamentous algae from live rock throughout the day. Generally advised to supplement with dried nori, spirulina-based prepared foods, and herbivore-formulated frozen foods, especially in tanks without abundant natural algae. Has been reported to accept occasional meaty items such as mysis and brine shrimp once settled, but a steady algae supply is essential to long-term health.

avoidWith: other Salarias or similar-shaped combtooth blennies
Acropora and other small-polyp stony corals it may nip

summary: A small algae-grazing combtooth blenny from Western Central Pacific lagoon reefs, with a reddish-brown body marked by green to white vertical bars. Generally peaceful but territorial toward similar blennies, and a known jumper.
careNotes: Best housed in a mature tank with abundant live rock that has had time to develop microalgae and filamentous algae growth; tanks scrubbed too clean tend to under-feed this species and it has been reported to slowly starve without a steady algae supply. Generally peaceful toward unlike tankmates but has been reported to be territorial toward conspecifics and similar-shaped blennies — typically kept singly, or as a simultaneously-introduced mated pair in larger systems. Commonly considered reef-safe, but isolated reports of nipping at Acropora and other SPS polyps and bases have been noted, especially in lightly stocked SPS systems or when algae is scarce; observe in mixed-coral setups. Like most blennies, has been reported to be a strong jumper, so a tightly fitted lid is generally advised. Source spread on care level: LiveAquaria and AquariumDomain rate it 'easy', Quality Marine rates it 'moderate' — recorded as intermediate to reflect the algae-dependent diet and territoriality caveats. Source spread on minimum tank size: all consulted retailers cite 30 US gal (~114 L); rounded to 120 L as the nearest nice-value to give this active grazer adequate rock surface for foraging. Source spread on adult size: LiveAquaria, AquariumDomain, Quality Marine 4 in (~10 cm), FishBase 11 cm TL — recorded as 9-11 cm. Lifespan is reported by Saltwater Fish as 4-6 years; other retailer pages did not specify and the figure should be treated as approximate. Saltwater Fish lists diet as omnivorous with both algae and small meaty foods; the species is primarily a herbivorous grazer per LiveAquaria and Quality Marine, so recorded as herbivore with diet notes capturing the meaty supplementation.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing; eggs are demersal and adhesive, attached by filamentous pads to a substrate, with planktonic larvae found in shallow coastal waters. Has rarely been bred in home aquariums, and rearing the planktonic larvae through metamorphosis is generally considered difficult and not routinely accomplished.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry reference: family Blenniidae, max 4 in, min 30 gal, temp 72-78 F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12, peaceful but may conflict with similar-shaped tankmates, reef-compatible with caveat (may nibble SPS polyps if underfed), herbivore grazer needing established algae growth, requires tight-fitting lid.

additional.name: theaquariumwiki.com
additional.notes: Designated secondary returned access-denied (Anubis security challenge). Cross-verification was performed using the alternate secondaries below.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Taxonomy and wild biology cross-check: Family Blenniidae, Order Blenniiformes, max 11 cm TL, Western Central Pacific (Indonesia, New Guinea, Solomon Islands, Palau), depth 0-12 m, preferred wild temperature 27.4-29.3 C (mean 28.8 C), oviparous with distinct pairing, demersal adhesive eggs, planktonic larvae, IUCN Least Concern.

additional.name: AquariumDomain
additional.notes: Third independent confirmation: 4 in adult, min 30 gal, temp 72-80 F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12, easy care, peaceful (conspecific-territorial), reef-compatible with SPS-nipping caveat, herbivore.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-check: 4 in adult, min 30 gal, temp 72-78 F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, dKH 8-12, moderate care, territorial toward own kind, reef-compatible, herbivore with omnivorous tendencies, often found in empty barnacle shells and mollusc tubes.

additional.name: Saltwater Fish
additional.notes: Cross-check for lifespan (4-6 years) and dietary notes (algae plus small meaty supplements like mysis/brine shrimp and marine pellets). Confirms 4 in adult, 30 gal min, standard reef water parameters, reef-safe with Acropora-nipping caveat.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing; demersal adhesive eggs and planktonic larvae rarely reared in home aquariums.
